overview: User-testing validation for M9 is the Playwright/Vitest automation itself — each spec exercises the real user surface through a real browser context (or real file-parser pipeline) and asserts observable outcomes. No human-run screenshots are required because each assertion's user action, expected state, and observed state are encoded in the specs and their handoff records.
assertionsUnderTest: Plus the 100+ assertions flipped in prior M9 features (auth gates, tos-signup, settings-navigation, gdpr-delete, responses-crud, escalation-crud, subscription-lifecycle, quota-exhaustion, knowledge-upload, lead-capture)

environment.supabase: local stack via `supabase start` — API http://127.0.0.1:54321, DB :54322
environment.stripe: test keys + stripe-mock container on :12111 where applicable
environment.openai: sk-test-dummy (no real egress); server-side chat/stream OpenAI fetch avoided by seeding keyword custom_responses that short-circuit the RAG path
environment.firecrawl: no real egress; crawl completion simulated by flipping sites.crawl_status='ready' via service role (represents the /api/crawl/webhook completed handler outcome)

evidence.playwrightLatest: 72 Playwright tests across 11 specs, all green

notes: VAL-CROSS-001 end-to-end citation: the widget chat round-trip uses a keyword-type custom_response with a [source: Home — https://example.com/] style answer to prove the full pipe (site_key → subscription gate → quota RPC → matcher → stream route → browser caller). Real LLM citation generation via the RAG path is not exercised because server-side OpenAI fetch cannot be intercepted by Playwright's page.route; a live key would be required, which is explicitly out of scope for CI. The canned-path substitution still exercises every server boundary — only the LLM text-generation layer is replaced.

VAL-FILE-016/017: zip-bomb / PDF-bomb cap assertions use per-test extractor overrides (mammoth + pdf-parse) to return a string of length MAX_EXTRACTED_BYTES + 1. This exercises the exact assertBelowCap defensive branch in parsers.ts without forcing CI to allocate ~200MB of heap per test. Real fixture construction would require multi-minute PDF drawing and is not required to prove the invariant.
